mnemonics: S.T.E.G.O. (Steganography Techniques Embed Gems Obscurely) - Remember that steganography hides information within other files, like a gem hidden in plain sight.
L.S.B. (Least Significant Bit) - Think 'Little Secret Bits' to recall that data is hidden in the least important bits of a cover file.
W.A.T.E.R.M.A.R.K. (Woven Artfully To Establish Rights, Marking Authenticity & Kinship) - A mnemonic for the purpose and method of digital watermarking.
keyPoints: Steganography is the practice of concealing a file, message, image, or video within another file, message, image, or video.
The goal of information hiding is often covert communication, making the existence of the information undetectable, unlike cryptography which only obscures content.
Digital watermarking is a technique to embed a covert, persistent marker into digital data to prove ownership or authenticity.
commonMistakes: Confusing steganography (hiding existence of data) with cryptography (scrambling data content). They are complementary, not the same.
Assuming information hiding techniques are always robust; many simple steganography methods can be detected or broken, rendering the hidden data insecure.
Overlooking the trade-off between the amount of data hidden (payload) and the perceptible degradation it causes to the cover medium.
